property.name: 17477a69-d9a5-4251-9329-0963e8121b5f
property.value:
Do not interpret “service” as only the Intent Service element; treat “service” as “endpoint surface”, which may be CQRS or Traditional
## Service diagraming guidance - Place entities 200px to the right of services (i.e. 200px gap) - DTO's cannot be added to diagrams
## Service Endpoint Distribution / Exposure modeling guidance - Exposure is optional. Default to internal application services unless external exposure is explicitly indicated by the user or existing model context. - Only apply exposure stereotypes when an endpoint is intended to be externally distributed. - CQRS: apply the appropriate exposure stereotype to the Command/Query. - Traditional Service: apply the appropriate exposure stereotype to the Operation (optionally configure base route on the Service). - Never assume the transport. If external exposure is intended but the transport is not stated, ask which (HTTP/REST, gRPC, GraphQL, messaging, etc.) before applying stereotypes. - When HTTP/REST is chosen/indicated: apply Http Settings (Verb + Route) to the exposed endpoint element.
## Modeling Endpoints on compositional entities guidance - Intent implies Aggregate Roots as follows: Aggregate Root = Class with no required-single parent association (not owned by exactly one parent). - If an endpoint/Command’s purpose is to create/update/delete a compositional child, model the Domain Interaction association as Create/Update/Delete Entity Action against the child entity. - If an endpoint/Command’s purpose is to create/update/delete an aggregate root, model ONLY a single Entity Action against that aggregate root. Do NOT add additional Entity Actions for compositional/supporting entities (e.g., tokens, audit records, outbox, child rows) that are created/updated/deleted as part of that aggregate operation; these are implied by the aggregate action. - Do not model compositional child CRUD as Update Entity Action against the owning aggregate root (even if the handler loads the owner aggregate to perform the change). - Mapping: Add<Child> ⇒ Create Entity Action → Child; Update<Child>/SetDefault<Child> ⇒ Update Entity Action → Child; Delete<Child> ⇒ Delete Entity Action → Child. - If the child does not have the Repository stereotype: the contract must include the owning aggregate id(s) required to load the owner (e.g., OrderId), plus any child id (e.g., OrderLineId) where applicable. - If the child does have the Repository stereotype: model it as acting directly on the child; owner ids are not required purely for loading the owner.
